root_text.an3.2:1.1: “Kammalakkhaṇo, bhikkhave, bālo, kammalakkhaṇo paṇḍito, apadānasobhanī paññāti.
root_text.an3.2:1.2: Tīhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bālo veditabbo.
root_text.an3.2:1.5: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, tīhi dhammehi samannāgato bālo veditabbo.
root_text.an3.2:2.1: Tīhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato paṇḍito veditabbo.
root_text.an3.2:2.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, tīhi dhammehi samannāgato paṇḍito veditabbo.
root_text.an3.2:3.2: ‘yehi tīhi dhammehi samannāgato bālo veditabbo te tayo dhamme abhinivajjetvā, yehi tīhi dhammehi samannāgato paṇḍito veditabbo te tayo dhamme samādāya vattissāmā’ti.

translation_text.an3.2:1.1: “A fool is characterized by their deeds, and an astute person is characterized by their deeds, for wisdom shines in its traces.
translation_text.an3.2:1.2: A fool is known by three things.
translation_text.an3.2:1.4: Bad conduct by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an3.2:1.5: These are the three things by which a fool is known.
translation_text.an3.2:2.1: An astute person is known by three things.
translation_text.an3.2:2.3: Good conduct by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an3.2:2.4: These are the three things by which an astute person is known.
translation_text.an3.2:3.1: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an3.2:3.2: ‘We will shun the three things by which a fool is known, and we will undertake and follow the three things by which an astute person is known.’

variant_text.an3.2:1.1: apadānasobhanī → apadānasobhinī (bj); apadāne sobhati (sya-all, km, pts1ed) | paññāti → paññā (sya-all); paññatti (?)
